text: Let \(S\) piecewise smooth oriented surface space, let boundary \(S\) piecewise smooth simple closed curve \(C\). \(\mathbf{F}(x, y, z)\) continuous vector function continuous first partial derivatives domain containing \(S\), :\[\iint_S (\text{curl } \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\]formula:\(\mathbf{n}\) unit normal vector \(S\).\(\mathbf{r}'(s)\) unit tangent vector \(C\).\(s\) arc length \(C\).integration around \(C\) performed sense consistent orientation normal vector \(\mathbf{n}\), following right-hand rule.

heading: 3.0.3 Double Integral
text:
double integral defined following identity:
\[\iint_R f(x, y) \, dA = \lim_{m, n \\infty} \sum_{=1}^m \sum_{j=1}^n f(x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*) \Delta \]\(R\) closed bounded region \(xy\)-plane function integrated [1, 2].\(m\) \(n\) number subrectangles region \(R\) partitioned [3, 4].\(R_{ij}\) smaller subrectangles resulting dividing \(R\) using grid lines parallel axes [4, 5].\(\Delta \) represents area subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].\((x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*)\) arbitrary sample point chosen within subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].limit taken \(m\) \(n\) approach infinity maximum diameter (greatest distance two points within subregion) approaches zero [3, 7].function \(f(x, y)\), integrand, typically assumed continuous \(R\) ensure limit exists independent choice sample points [7, 8].

heading: 3.0.4 Double Integral Notational Variations
text:
exist double integral two equivalent notations:
* Differential Area Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dA\).
* Coordinate Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dx \, dy\).

heading: 3.0.5 Projection
text: definition (vector projection) vector representing “shadow” vector \(\mathbf{b}\) cast onto line containing vector \(\mathbf{}\), denoted \(\text{proj}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b}\). Geometrically, vector common initial point foot perpendicular dropped tip \(\mathbf{b}\) onto line containing \(\mathbf{}\). calculated using following formula:\(\text{proj}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = \left( \dfrac{\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}}{|\mathbf{}|^2} \right) \mathbf{}\)\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{b}\) vectors coordinate system.\(\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}\) dot product two vectors.\(|\mathbf{}|\) magnitude vector \(\mathbf{}\).definition (scalar projection) signed magnitude vector projection \(\mathbf{b}\) onto \(\mathbf{}\), also referred component \(\mathbf{b}\) along \(\mathbf{}\), denoted \(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b}\). represents length “shadow” positive angle vectors acute negative obtuse. defined following formulas:\(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = \dfrac{\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}}{|\mathbf{}|}\)\(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = |\mathbf{b}| \cos \theta\)\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{b}\) vectors coordinate system.\(\theta\) angle vectors \(\mathbf{}\) \(\mathbf{b}\).\(|\mathbf{b}|\) magnitude vector \(\mathbf{b}\).
